task: Update Datalox docs so this repo focuses on the B2B trajectory data/eval product, while keeping desktop agent skill/note improvement as supporting capture infrastructure.
step: refocus docs on B2B trajectory data product
summary: Refocused canonical docs and instruction surfaces so the repo now presents Datalox Trajectory Data as the primary B2B dataset/eval product, with Datalox MCP and local skill/note reuse framed as capture, consent, verification, and quality infrastructure.
observations: docs/product-definition.md now defines Datalox Trajectory Data as the primary B2B product and separates the supporting local trace->note->skill loop from the primary export loop.
README, START_HERE, DATALOX, TODO, project overview, agent configuration, and host instruction surfaces now say B2B trajectory data/evals are the primary product focus.
The old equal-weight direct-user-value vs commercial-value wording was removed from visible docs.
title: Refocused canonical docs and instruction surfaces so the repo now presents Datalox Trajectory Data as the primary B2B d…
signal: User clarified that repo docs should focus more on the B2B version and not mix direct user value with commercial data value as equal product promises.
interpretation: The repo should treat Datalox Trajectory Data/Evals as the primary product, while keeping desktop-agent skill/note behavior as supporting infrastructure for capture quality.
recommendedAction: Next implementation work should prioritize the trajectory export command/schema validation over local user-facing agent features.
outcome: npm run build passed. npm test passed: 13 test files, 122 tests. Datalox lint returned ok:true with 24 existing warning-level note/reference issues.
